label: Literary Analysis & Close Reading
vocabulary: narrative voice and point of view

sources.title: Writing About Literature: Writing About Love

app_prompts: Close reading practice: user pastes a paragraph of fiction and gets prompts to identify narrative voice, point of view, figurative language, and what the passage reveals about character or theme.
Literary theory lens selector: user picks a text and a critical lens — Marxist, psychoanalytic, feminist, postcolonial — and sees what questions each approach would ask of the same passage.

Postcolonial reading guide: user selects a canonical text (Conrad, Kipling, Forster) and sees what a postcolonial reading reveals — Orientalism, the civilizing mission, the subaltern perspective missing from the narrative.
Genre identification quiz: user reads an excerpt and identifies the genre markers — Gothic, Bildungsroman, modernist stream of consciousness — and explains what conventions the text uses.

Feminist literary criticism explainer: user picks a text and traces how feminist critics have read it differently than the original canon — what was ignored, what was recovered, whose perspective was centered.
